summary: 0037 field strand (slice 4): `explain` reads the tap log and narrates event counts + denominators joined to members through the lock — evidence narrated never judged, older records counted not skipped

edit.description: The `field` strand — a fourth member-grain traversal beside `why` (~282), `impact` (~548), `context` (~824), narrating the per-machine tap record for a named member. Add `pub fn field(...)` and append it in `explain`'s `Species::Member` arm (~226-247, after the `context` push) — a read, never a gate, so the caller still exits zero on every target (the module's exit-zero contract, ~194). It takes the parsed tap records + older-version count threaded in from main.rs (the `crate::tap::read_log` output) plus the member's identity, and narrates that member's event counts + denominators joined through the SAME lock declarations the gate reads (`by_kind`/roster inputs already carried, READ-EDGE-UNIFY — the strand cannot disagree with a green `check`): evidence narrated, never judged, never mined for model structure (pipeline.md 'Telemetry'; contract.md 'Read verbs'). The older-`TAP_RECORD_VERSION` count `read_log` returns surfaces as a line in the narration — a count, never a silent skip; an absent/empty log narrates no field strand at all. Widen `pub fn explain` (~211) with the records+count argument and thread it to the new strand.

The `explain` assembly (~483-577) reads the tap log via `crate::tap::read_log` at the `pub(crate)` log-path locator `src/tap.rs` owns (records + older-version count), and threads the pair into `read::explain` (~573) as its new argument — the same shape the field strand narrates. An absent log yields an empty read, not an error (the strand narrates none). Region disjoint from TAP-VERB's `Command`/`Tap` dispatch (~180/~354) and EMBEDDED-EDGE-SOURCE-SEAM (~2043) on this file — serialized behind TAP-VERB via blockedBy, no logic dep.

Field strand fixtures via the existing `explain` test helper (~65 — thread the new tap-records+count argument through it, keeping every current call site green). Assert: a member-target `explain` over a log whose records name that member narrates its event counts + denominators joined through the lock; a record an older `TAP_RECORD_VERSION` wrote surfaces as a count in the narration (never a silent skip, never a read failure); an absent log narrates no field strand and `explain` still exits zero. Write the log through `crate::tap`'s append writer (TAP-VERB) so the fixture drives the real record shape (`engineering.md` 'One job, one home' — extend explain's own test home, no new file). Overlaps VERIFIER-TYPED on this file (its mechanical `verified_by`->`verifier` fixture rename ~52 vs. this file's explain helper ~65 + new field-strand fns) — disjoint regions, serialized by their distinct blockedBy gates.

tests.asserts: a member-target `explain` narrates a field strand — the member's tap-event counts + denominators joined through the lock; an older-version record is counted in the narration not silently skipped; an absent log narrates no field strand and `explain` exits zero on every target

acceptance: `explain` narrates a field strand beside why/impact/context — the per-machine tap log's event counts and denominators joined to members through the same lock declarations the gate reads, evidence narrated never judged; a record an older tap wrote surfaces as a count in the narration, an absent log narrates none, and `explain` never gates and exits zero (contract.md, 'Read verbs'; 0037 amended). `cargo test` green.
notes: 0037 slice 4 of 5 (amended 5b6b6f2): the tap record's sole reader. Type + writer + version-tolerant read_log live in TAP-VERB's src/tap.rs (one home for record IO); this slice is the pure explain-side consumer — no src/tap.rs edit, so the references-resolve gate holds (the prior attempts scoped the reader into the not-yet-created src/tap.rs and reverted). Joins events to members through the lock as the gate reads them (READ-EDGE-UNIFY), never mined for structure. blockedBy TAP-VERB.

summary: Derive 0037's verifier-type resolution: retype `verified_by` into a species union — script (path) + telemetry (documented event names), each resolved at admissibility; probe deferred

per.section: requirement — a shipped kind, not a primitive

edit.description: Retype the verifier slot. `Requirement.verifiedBy?: string` (~284) becomes `verifier?: Verifier`, a discriminated union with two active species this slice — add `Verifier` plus the `script(path: string)` and `telemetry(events: readonly string[])` constructors (both erase to `{ species, ... }`). PROBE IS NOT TYPED HERE: 0037 keeps probe a documented pattern until its transcript surface is documented or a second consumer types it — do not mint a `probe()` constructor. The `telemetry` constructor documents its admissible event names (InstructionsLoaded, Skill, UserPromptExpansion, PostToolUse — code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks, retrieved 2026-07-17 per 0037; build re-verifies against live docs at encode). Reword the interface doc (~277) `verifiedBy wires the behavioral remainder` to the typed verifier. EXTENT shipped 4bdb796 and shifted this file (verifier slot now ~300 not ~284); no open entry shares contract.ts.

The requirement lowering (~343) currently emits `verified_by: requirement.verifiedBy`; emit the species-tagged verifier onto the wire row instead (the `verifier` column carrying species + payload). Snake_case wire keys to match the Rust `#[derive(Deserialize)]` reader (`sdk.md`, 'The engine seam'). STAR-SEGMENT-LOCUS shipped 2a89535 (its unit-shape region settled on disk) — disjoint from this requirement-lowering region ~344. (EXTENT shipped 4bdb796.)

Regenerated once the Rust `RequirementRow` verifier column lands: `verified_by?: string` (~36) becomes the typed `verifier?` column mirroring the new `Verifier` shape. Generated seam, not hand-authored — run the SDK generate step and commit the diff. If the Rust `Verifier` enum derives `TS` as a standalone type, the generate step also emits a NEW `sdk/src/generated/Verifier.ts` and its `generated/index.ts` re-export — commit those too; if the wire shape inlines into RequirementRow, no new file appears. Build confirms off the derive.

If the root barrel re-exports the contract constructors, add `script`/`telemetry` and the `Verifier` type beside the existing requirement surface; drop nothing else. Confirm on disk whether contract constructors are re-exported here before editing.

The in-engine model. `Requirement.verified_by: Option<String>` (~100) becomes `verifier: Option<Verifier>`, a new `enum Verifier { Script { path: String }, Telemetry { events: Vec<String> } }` — two variants this slice, probe deferred. Reword the field doc (~98-99) to the typed verifier. `Verifier` is the shared type the drift row lifts into and roster resolves over.

The wire/lock row. `RequirementRow.verified_by: Option<String>` (~2877, the `ts_rs::TS` derive that sources the generated TS) becomes the typed `verifier` column (a nested serde-tagged verifier, `#[serde(default)]`); its `to_table` serialization (~3725-3727) writes the species sub-table and `from_table` (~3751) reads it back. MANIFEST-SEGMENT-REAP (2a2d583), STAR-SEGMENT-LOCUS (2a89535), and EMBEDDED-EXTENT-CAPTURE (7cf9ff0) shipped (reap-ledger / splice-name / NestedMemberRow span-column regions settled on disk) — all struct-disjoint from this RequirementRow region. (EXTENT shipped 4bdb796.)
`requirement_from_row` (~2399) reconstructs `compose::Requirement` from the row: carry the typed verifier across (`verifier: row.verifier...`) instead of `verified_by: row.verified_by.clone()`.

The sole resolution/admissibility check. Clause (b) (~127-138) currently tests one path; dispatch per species: `Script { path }` resolves as today (`base_dir.join(path).exists()`, the dangling-verifier message), `Telemetry { events }` resolves each named event against the documented harness-event name set (an unknown event name is inadmissible — a telemetry verifier that names an undocumented event is a silent no-op, same failure shape). The documented event-name set is an external fact carried here (or a small shared const) with its cite (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks, retrieved 2026-07-17; build re-verifies). Rename the doc at ~94 and the field access. Migrate the two unit tests (~640-658) to the typed verifier and add a telemetry case. RIDE-ONLY: while this file is open, cut the orphan `10-contracts.md` cite in the `membership_roster` helper doc (~475, a file 0001 deleted) — the sentence's claim stays, only the dead source pointer comes out (`rust.md`, spec citations retired from comments).
Mechanical field-rename ripple: the `verified_by: None` requirement fixture (~1487) becomes `verifier: None`.
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~1693) -> `verifier: None`.
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~172) -> `verifier: None`.

The verifier admissibility fixtures migrate and extend. `a_requirement_with_a_dangling_verified_by_is_inadmissible` (~388) and `a_roster_whose_verifiers_all_resolve_passes` (~418) re-spell `verified_by: Some(path)` as `verifier: Some(script(path))`; ADD telemetry cases: a requirement whose telemetry verifier names a documented event passes admissibility, and one naming an undocumented event is inadmissible with the no-op message. This is the entry's primary behavioral fixture (extending the existing home, `engineering.md` 'One job, one home' — no new file).
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~52) -> `verifier: None`.
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~126) -> `verifier: None`.
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~261) -> `verifier: None`.
The `verifiedBy: "tests/dev-standards.test.ts"` fixture (~190) re-spells as `verifier: script("tests/dev-standards.test.ts")`; its emitted-row assertion follows the new `verifier` wire shape.

Add SDK coverage for the typed union: `script(path)` and `telemetry(events)` produce the expected species-tagged clause/row values, and a round-trip proves the wire key matches the Rust reader. If no verifier coverage exists today, this is its home.

tests.asserts: a script verifier resolves like today (path exists passes, dangling is inadmissible); a telemetry verifier naming a documented harness event passes admissibility and one naming an undocumented event is inadmissible; both species round-trip through the lock row
the `script` and `telemetry` constructors produce the expected species-tagged verifier wire values

acceptance: The requirement's verifier is a typed species union: `script(path)` (today's path resolution, retyped) and `telemetry(events)` (named documented harness events), each resolved at admissibility — a script's path exists, a telemetry event's name is a documented one, and either failing is inadmissible with the silent-no-op finding (contract.md, 'requirement': the gate checks the declaration resolves, never runs it; no verifier verdict enters check's exit code). Probe is not typed this slice (0037 keeps it a documented pattern). `pnpm --dir sdk test` + `cargo test` green.
notes: 0037 slice 5 of 5 (6d2cca6): verifier-type resolution. Un-derived siblings, one slice per tick (cursor holds 63e1f22): tap verb, telemetry declaration+hook projection, local-locus log kind, field strand. This slice owns the documented event-NAME set (telemetry's resolution target); event PAYLOAD cites land with their consumer downstream. Probe deferred per 0037. EXTENT shipped 4bdb796 → open; region-disjoint on drift.rs/main.rs/graph.rs/declarations.ts. scoped at f19b052.

summary: Derive 0037's telemetry hook projection: emit synthesizes deduped tap `hooks.<Event>` rows from a telemetry verifier — one per lifecycle event, matcher+command cited

edit.description: The synthesis home. Add `tapHookRows(harness)` beside `registrationRows` (~627): scan the same requirement sources `requirementRows` reads — `harness.require` (~330) and each member's `member.requires` (~332) — filter requirements whose typed verifier (VERIFIER-TYPED) is the telemetry species (`requirement.verifier` matching `Verifier.Telemetry`), and UNION the lifecycle events they name into ONE `hook` `RegistrationRow` per event — deduped: the tap is dumb, one registration per event however many verifiers name it (the permission-union derived-aggregate precedent, `needs.ts`:39 / pipeline.md 'Telemetry'). Each row is `{ kind: 'hook', key: <settings.json lifecycle event>, manifest: 'settings.json', key_path: 'hooks.<Event>', fields: [['type','command'], ['command', <the tap invocation, slice [1] `temper tap`>], ['matcher', <the documented matcher for this event>]] }` (the hook field shape, `sdk/test/emit.test.ts`:1430). Owns the event-NAME -> (lifecycle event, matcher) mapping and its cites, re-verified against live docs at encode (external-fact bar): `InstructionsLoaded` (rule/memory load, the load reason / `path_glob_match` its matcher), `Skill` -> `PostToolUse` with `tool_name:'Skill'` (skill invocation), `UserPromptExpansion` (command names), `PostToolUse` — code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks, retrieved 2026-07-17 per 0037. Merge into the registrations family in `compileDeclarations` (~747): `registrations: [...registrationRows(harness), ...tapHookRows(harness)]`. Overlaps VERIFIER-TYPED (requirement-lowering region, `verifiedBy`->`verifier` at ~343) — serialized behind it.

The public `EmitResult` registration view follows: `registrationFacts(harness)` (~591) enumerates registrations from members only, so the memberless synthesized tap hooks are absent from `EmitResult.registrations` (~679/~710) unless merged. Fold the same `tapHookRows` (or its `RegistrationFact` projection) in beside `registrationFacts(harness)` at ~710 so the returned view matches the seam the engine projects. Confirm the fact shape; minimal beyond the merge.

The primary behavioral fixture: a harness whose requirement carries a telemetry verifier naming two lifecycle events (e.g. `InstructionsLoaded` + `Skill`) emits the expected tap `hooks.<Event>` rows into settings.json — one registration per lifecycle event, DEDUPED across two verifiers naming the same event, each with the documented matcher + the tap command. Model on the existing hook-projection fixture (~1430-1468, the `hooks` segment + residue assertions). Overlaps VERIFIER-TYPED (which retypes the `verifiedBy`->`verifier` fixture ~190) — serialized behind it.

The cross-language seam assertion (`sdk.md`, 'The engine seam'): a payload whose `declarations.registrations` carries a synthesized tap-hook `RegistrationRow` (kind hook, a `matcher` field, `temper tap` command) projects the matcher group into the emitted `.claude/settings.json` `hooks.<Event>` array via the engine's registration loop (`src/drift.rs`:932 -> `hook_matcher_group` `src/extract.rs`:1127) — proving the engine projects the SDK-synthesized row unchanged. Add only if the matcher-group projection off a `matcher` field is not already exercised here; confirm existing coverage first (`engineering.md` 'One job, one home').

tests.asserts: a requirement's telemetry verifier projects one tap `hooks.<Event>` registration per named lifecycle event, deduped across verifiers naming the same event, each carrying the documented matcher and the tap command

the engine projects a synthesized tap-hook RegistrationRow's matcher group into `.claude/settings.json` hooks.<Event> unchanged — the SDK-side synthesis rides the existing registration projection across the seam

acceptance: A requirement's telemetry verifier projects at emit as tap `hooks.<Event>` registrations — one deduped hook per lifecycle event any telemetry verifier names, matcher shaped per the documented event (`path_glob_match` for InstructionsLoaded, `tool_name:'Skill'` under PostToolUse for skill invocation, UserPromptExpansion for commands; each cited code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks, re-verified at encode), command the tap invocation, riding the registration family into settings.json 'the same rows any hook rides' (pipeline.md, 'Telemetry'). The rows carry through the seam and the engine projects them via its existing registration loop, no engine change. `pnpm --dir sdk test` + `cargo test` green.
notes: 0037 slice 2 of 5 (6d2cca6): telemetry's hook projection. SDK-side synthesis (tapHookRows beside registrationRows) reusing the engine's registration loop (drift.rs:932) unchanged. blockedBy VERIFIER-TYPED: needs the typed verifier + overlaps declarations.ts/emit.test.ts. No builtin_lock touch (no shipped telemetry verifier) -> disjoint from EXTENT/SETTINGS-LOCAL. command targets sibling tap [1]; [3] (log format open at derive)/[4] un-derived, cursor holds. scoped at 5b81ba8.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (630) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (657), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 528 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 626-629 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 652-655 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). 6667265 (mention-reachable, in the b85df4a..HEAD window) shifted every address after ~519 — the const (59) and its doc (55-58) sit above the edit and are unmoved; the later addresses re-read at HEAD, so every address carries.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1407) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1408-1412) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1410) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. 6667265 (mention-reachable, in the b85df4a..HEAD window) added the mention-reachable fixture and shifted this test down; addresses re-read at HEAD, so they carry.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 0349821 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. 6667265 (mention-reachable) touched both files this window and shifted the later addresses; re-read at HEAD, all carry. Constant still 5 on disk (`graph.rs`:59) — the park's subject is untouched, and may still be wrong. A build-side friction capture records the same disagreement; the human's to read, not plan's to drain. rescoped 4a33911; park re-tested 0349821.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 0349821, holding on every clause: no version tag exists, crate 0.1.0, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff b85df4a..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
